text.title: viral-loops Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for viral-loops as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent recommends "make it inherent" for a non-collaborative product.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Forced inherent virality creates fake invite prompts that users reject. It kills NPS without raising K. Inherent virality only works when collaboration is the core value proposition.

failure-mode.description: Computing K on fewer than 500 invite events and reporting it as meaningful.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: K is volatile at small samples. Signal:noise ratio is unacceptable below ~500 invites per reporting period. Agent must flag low-power computations explicitly.

failure-mode.description: Recommending outbreak/spam loops based on outdated case studies.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pre-2005 outbreak mechanics are blocked by modern email filters and mobile OS permissions. Always reject this suggestion regardless of source.

failure-mode.description: Models confuse K with conversion rate; force the prompt to specify K = i times c.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: K is the product of average invites per user (i) and invitee conversion rate (c). Conflating K with c leads to wrong optimization decisions.

failure-mode.description: Agent pushing outbreak (auto-contact-import) loops without explicit human approval.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: TOS and CAN-SPAM landmine. Outbreak mechanics are dead on modern email and mobile platforms.

failure-mode.description: Weekly K computed on fewer than 500 invites reported as meaningful signal.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: K is noise below ~500 invites per reporting period; agent should flag low-power computations rather than report them.

failure-mode.description: Agents over-fit on Dropbox/PayPal case studies when recommending a target K.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require it to cite a benchmark range (consumer apps: 0.1-0.3; B2B SaaS: 0.05-0.2) before recommending a target K.

failure-mode.description: Mobile attribution agents using different client_id across web and app.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Passing different IDs silently breaks K attribution. Use the same client_id consistently across all platforms.
